content:
I experienced this issue while writing a patch and I managed to just figure out the problem by staring at the source code. I forgot to delete an extraneous ) in a doctest:

//! ```
//! fn foo() {
//!     Ok())
//! }
//! ```

This strangely results in a very unhelpful error message:

$ make LLVM=1 -j16
mkdir -p .../tools/objtool && make O=... subdir=tools/objtool --no-print-directory -C objtool
  CALL    scripts/checksyscalls.sh
  INSTALL libsubcmd_headers
  RUSTC L rust/kernel.o
  EXPORTS rust/exports_kernel_generated.h
  RUSTDOC TK rust/kernel/lib.rs
make[2]: *** [rust/Makefile:178: rust/doctests_kernel_generated.rs] Error 101
make[1]: *** [.../Makefile:1209: prepare] Error 2
make: *** [Makefile:224: __sub-make] Error 2

I have CONFIG_RUST_KERNEL_DOCTESTS=y. When I have a different syntax error such as this:

//! ```
//! fn
//! ```

Then the error is much more reasonable:

  RUSTC     rust/doctests_kernel_generated.o
error: expected identifier, found `}`
    --> rust/doctests_kernel_generated.rs:1893:1
     |
1893 | } _doctest_main_rust_kernel_lib_rs_14_0() }
     | ^ expected identifier

Any idea why that is and what we can do to prevent this?
